scenes.text: The prince carried the sacred gamelan to Mbok Rondo's humble home. The golden snail was placed gently on the floor. The village fell silent, as if the world itself was holding its breath.

Raden Inu began to play. The melody was soft yet powerful sorrowful yet full of hope. It echoed through the walls, through the trees, through the river where Candra Kirana had once drifted in darkness.
The golden shell began to glow, then cracked open. Light poured out like sunrise. Where the snail once lay, Candra Kirana now stood radiant, whole, and free.

Their eyes met. No words were needed. The kingdom rejoiced, Mbok Rondo was welcomed as family, and the tale of the golden snail became a story passed down for generations a reminder that love, kindness, and patience can break even the darkest of curses.
